loops.description: Bounded converge and host-configuration workflows repair declared infrastructure by replaying the repo-managed reconcile path before any broader rollback or replacement step.
loops.invariant: The governed host or service converge path leaves repo-managed infrastructure aligned with inventory intent and any declared post-verify probe healthy.
observation_sources: The workflow exit status and task-level change set from the bounded Ansible entrypoint.
The shared post-verify health-probe contract under config/health-probe-catalog.json.
Inventory, controller-local secrets, and live host or guest state gathered by the playbook preflight.

repair_actions.summary: Record evidence when the declared service or host state is already healthy and no reconcile is needed.
Replay the same repo-managed converge path because the contract is intended to be idempotent.
Use the service-specific rollback, failback, or immutable replacement path when a known-good state is still recoverable.
Escalate before any destructive mutation or when the reconcile path cannot prove the repair is safe.

verification.source: workflow verification_commands plus shared post-verify health probes
verification.success_signal: The converge command exits cleanly and the declared health or readiness probe reports a healthy target.

escalation.boundary: Stop when replay would exceed the bounded idempotent contract, a destructive replacement is required, or the target is not safe for autonomous mutation.

loops.description: Provider API, DNS, publication, dashboard, and maintenance-window mutations reconcile an external control surface by replaying the governed request before any manual intervention.
loops.invariant: The targeted external control surface reflects the declared repo state or approved operator request, and the affected service still matches its published contract.
observation_sources: The provider or control-plane API response for the governed mutation.
The generated receipt, command contract, or live-control export written by the workflow.
Any linked service-health or publication probe that proves the external state is actually usable.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the requested external state already matches the declared contract.
Replay the same governed external API call or publication helper with the current repo inputs.
Restore the previous known-good publication, DNS, or control-plane state when the external surface supports bounded rollback.
Escalate when the provider response is unsafe, the action is destructive, or the external state cannot be verified from the governed probes.

verification.source: Workflow-specific verification_commands plus any linked health or publication probes.
verification.success_signal: The external control surface returns the expected state and any affected service endpoint still satisfies its governed probe.

escalation.boundary: Stop when the external system rejects the request semantically, when rollback would delete state without prior approval, or when verification cannot prove the new state is usable.

loops.description: Identity and secret workflows repair one named principal or credential contract by replaying the governed sync or rotation path before escalating to a human.
loops.invariant: The authoritative roster or secret contract is converged across the approved identity authorities, controller-local artifacts, and downstream runtime consumers.
observation_sources: The repo-authoritative roster, token inventory, or secret catalog inputs for the named identity or credential.
The external identity or secret authority response returned by the workflow.
The governed inventory, whoami, or audit command used to prove the rotated or synchronized state.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the identity or credential contract already matches the current authoritative state.
Replay the same repo-managed sync or rotation path with the named identity or secret contract.
Restore the previous known-good principal or credential linkage only when the bounded rollback path is explicitly documented by the owning runbook.
Escalate when revocation, replacement, or consumer fallout could destroy access without a reviewed rollback plan.

verification.source: Workflow verification_commands plus controller-local inventory and whoami checks.
verification.success_signal: The authoritative identity or secret state and the governed verification command agree on the new principal or credential contract.

escalation.boundary: Stop when the repair would revoke access without a validated replacement path, or when a downstream consumer still points at stale credentials after bounded replay.

loops.description: Ephemeral fixture and warm-pool workflows reconcile lease, expiry, and pool-count drift with the governed fixture catalog before operators intervene.
loops.invariant: The ephemeral fleet matches the governed receipt retention, TTL, and warm-pool targets recorded in the repo catalogs.
observation_sources: Ephemeral fixture receipts and archived run state under receipts and .local.
The governed pool catalog and lease metadata.
The live Proxmox fixture inventory and summary receipts emitted by the reaper or reconcile job.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the fixture inventory already matches the governed receipt and pool contract.
Replay the bounded reaper or pool reconcile pass so the governed catalog can restore counts and archive the right evidence.
Destroy and refill one ephemeral member only when the fixture policy explicitly pre-approves that destructive correction path.
Escalate when a receipt cannot be trusted, a live lease is ambiguous, or destructive cleanup would exceed the governed fixture policy.

verification.source: Fixture receipts, pool-status output, and the reaper or reconcile summary receipt.
verification.success_signal: The generated summary receipt shows the expected expired, skipped, and replenished members with no unresolved lease ambiguity.

escalation.boundary: Stop when receipt parsing fails, when a fixture lease is still active, or when cleanup would destroy state that the policy does not pre-approve.

loops.description: Promotion and dependency-wave workflows repair batch or staged rollout drift by replaying the governed gate and converge path, not by bypassing evidence or approval surfaces.
loops.invariant: The staged or grouped rollout runs only through the governed evidence chain, leaves every targeted service healthy, and preserves a bounded rollback path.
observation_sources: Staging receipts, promotion receipts, and wave receipts referenced by the rollout.
The validation gate and command-approval outcome that authorize the batch.
The per-service verification commands and health probes executed after the rollout.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the staged or grouped rollout already completed and the evidence chain is still current.
Replay the governed promotion gate or dependency wave from the same bounded manifest and evidence set.
Use the batch rollback bundle, previous healthy receipt, or failback plan when the rollout must be unwound within the documented window.
Escalate when the staged evidence is stale, rollback would be destructive, or the grouped rollout leaves an unsafe partial state.

verification.source: The linked staging or wave receipts plus the targeted service health or verification commands.
verification.success_signal: Every targeted service passes its bounded verification slice and the final receipt chain is complete.

escalation.boundary: Stop when the evidence chain is stale, incomplete, or points at a partial rollout that lacks a documented rollback bundle.

loops.description: Repository-state publication workflows keep generated artifacts, package outputs, and merge-authored config state aligned with their canonical inputs before they affect live automation.
loops.invariant: The generated repo artifact or publication output matches the current canonical inputs and any downstream automation consumes the refreshed artifact rather than stale state.
observation_sources: Generated file diffs, publication receipts, or merge receipts emitted by the workflow.
The validating command or downstream repo check that consumes the refreshed artifact.
The governing catalog or manifest that defines the authoritative input set.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the generated or published repo surface already matches its canonical inputs.
Regenerate or republish the bounded artifact from the same canonical source of truth.
Restore the previous known-good generated artifact or publication bundle when the new output breaks a downstream contract.
Escalate when the generated state cannot be reproduced from the current repo inputs or when republishing would overwrite an unknown artifact.

verification.source: Workflow verification_commands plus the downstream validation gate that consumes the refreshed artifact.
verification.success_signal: The regenerated or republished artifact passes the same repo validation or publication health check that depends on it.

escalation.boundary: Stop when canonical inputs are inconsistent, when publication would overwrite untracked external state, or when the regenerated artifact fails the same downstream gate twice.

loops.description: Operational surface hygiene workflows regenerate bounded portal, discovery, and capacity-reporting artifacts from repo-managed truth before operators intervene.
loops.invariant: The generated operational surface matches the current repo catalogs and the bounded verification command confirms the refreshed artifact is usable.
observation_sources: Workflow-local generated files, rendered portal fragments, or JSON summaries emitted by the bounded command.
The repo catalogs and manifests that define the authoritative discovery, portal, or capacity inputs.
The verification command attached to the workflow that proves the refreshed operational surface is consumable.

repair_actions.summary: Record that the operational surface already matches the current repo truth and no refresh is needed.
Replay the bounded generation or reconciliation command so the operational surface is rebuilt from the same canonical inputs.
Escalate when the regenerated surface still fails verification or when republishing would overwrite an unknown or operator-managed artifact.

verification.source: The workflow verification_commands plus any downstream portal or artifact validation that consumes the regenerated surface.
verification.success_signal: The refreshed operational surface passes its bounded verification command without introducing new drift.

escalation.boundary: Stop when the same bounded refresh path fails twice, when canonical inputs disagree, or when a follow-up repair would leave the governed read-only artifact boundary.

loops.description: The watchdog and observation-loop runtimes are themselves governed correction loops: they must emit durable state, choose the narrowest safe repair action first, and stop at explicit safety boundaries.
loops.invariant: The live automation loop observes drift or stale execution, records the diagnosis and bounded repair path, and either proves the repair succeeded or escalates cleanly.
observation_sources: The incoming finding batch or running-job snapshot handled by the loop.
Durable loop-run state, scheduler heartbeat files, and watchdog action history written under .local/state.
The workflow-catalog verification block or world-state service-health entry used to prove the target recovered.

repair_actions.summary: Record evidence when the observation target is already healthy or the running job set is already within the bounded safety contract.
Replay the bounded observation or watchdog tick so the runtime can re-evaluate drift and dispatch the narrowest approved repair path.
Use the owning workflow rollback, failback, or previous-known-good state when the loop has evidence that the prior state is recoverable.
Escalate when the loop hits an unsafe mutation boundary, exhausts its bounded re-triage budget, or would need a destructive action that is not pre-approved by contract.

verification.source: The durable loop run record, watchdog heartbeat or action history, and the workflow or service-health verification probe chosen by the runtime.
verification.success_signal: The loop records a healthy no-op or a verified repair outcome and persists that evidence in its durable state.

escalation.boundary: Stop when repeated re-triage or stale-job recovery reaches the configured safety ceiling, or when a destructive mutation would require a separate approval path.
